perturbation.original_text: You agree to provide the Funds, upon written request, the taxpayer identification number ("TIN"), if known, of any or all Shareholder(s) of an account and the amount, date, name or other identifier of any registered representative(s) associated with the Shareholder(s) or account (if known), and transaction type (purchase, redemption, transfer, or exchange) of every purchase, redemption, transfer, or exchange of Shares held through an account maintained by you during the period covered by the request.
perturbation.changed_text: You agree to reasonably attempt to provide the Funds, upon written request, the taxpayer identification number ("TIN"), if available, of any Shareholder(s) of an account and the amount, date, name or other identifier of any registered representative(s) associated with the Shareholder(s) or account (if readily available), and transaction type of every transaction of Shares held through an account maintained by you during the period covered by the request.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing "if known" with "if available" and "if known" with "if readily available", and removing the requirement to provide information for *all* shareholders, the obligation becomes more ambiguous and less enforceable. It also undermines the fund's ability to track shareholder activity for regulatory compliance.
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 22c-2 under the Investment Company Act of 1940 requires funds to enter into agreements with intermediaries (like the Servicing Agent) to provide shareholder transaction information and to restrict trading by shareholders who violate fund policies against market timing. The modified text weakens the Servicing Agent's obligation to provide this information, hindering the Fund's ability to comply with Rule 22c-2 by making it harder to identify and restrict market timers.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Federal Register :: Request Access Request Access Request Access Due to aggressive automated scraping of FederalRegister.gov and eCFR.gov, programmatic access to these sites is limited to access to our extensive developer APIs. This process will be necessary for each IP address you wish to access the site from, requests are valid for approximately one quarter (three months) after which the process may need to be repeated. perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 270.22c-2 - Redemption fees for redeemable securities. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 270.22c-2 - Redemption fees for redeemable securities. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 270.22c-2 Redemption fees for redeemable securities. (a) Redemption fee. It is unlawful for any fund issuing redeemable securities, its principal underwriter , or any dealer in such securities, to redeem a redeemable security issued by the fund within seven calendar days after the security was purchased, unless it complies with the following requirements: (1) Board determination. The fund 's board of directors, including a majority of directors who are not interested persons of the fund , must either: (i) Approve a redemption fee, in an amount (but no more than two percent of the value of shares redeemed) and on shares redeemed within a time period (but no less than seven calendar days), that in its judgment is necessary or appropriate to recoup for the fund the costs it may incur as a result of those redemptions or to otherwise eliminate or reduce so far as practicable any dilution of the value of the outstanding securities issued by the fund , the proceeds of which fee will be retained by the fund ; or (ii) Determine that imposition of a redemption fee is either not necessary or not appropriate. (2) Shareholder information. With respect to each financial intermediary that submits orders, itself or through its agent, to purchase or redeem shares directly to the fund , its principal underwriter or transfer agent, or to a registered clearing agency, the fund (or on the fund 's behalf, the principal underwriter or transfer agent) must either: (i) Enter into a shareholder information agreement with the financial intermediary (or its agent); or (ii) Prohibit the financial intermediary from purchasing in nominee name on behalf of other persons, securities issued by th
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the obligation of the Servicing Agent to provide shareholder information, which contradicts Rule 22c-2 under the Investment Company Act of 1940. The original contract required the provision of taxpayer identification numbers (TINs) and other information 'if known,' whereas the modified version changes this to 'if available' or 'if readily available,' and removes the requirement to provide information for *all* shareholders. Scraped Law Snippet 2 refers to 17 CFR § 270.22c-2, concerning redemption fees for redeemable securities, and states in subsection (a)(2) that funds must enter into shareholder information agreements with financial intermediaries. By making the provision of information less mandatory, the modified text undermines the fund's ability to obtain necessary shareholder information, hindering compliance with Rule 22c-2.

perturbation.original_text: You agree to promptly and fully cooperate with any reasonable request made by the Fund to address market timing or excessive trading strategies identified by Nationwide in accordance with the applicable provisions of Rule 22c-2 and agree to restrict or prohibit further purchases or exchanges of Shares by a Shareholder that has been identified by such Fund as having engaged in transactions of the Fund's Shares (directly or indirectly through your account) that violate policies established by the Fund for the purpose of eliminating or reducing any dilution of the value of the outstanding Shares issued by the Fund.
perturbation.changed_text: You agree to cooperate with any request made by the Fund to address market timing or excessive trading strategies identified by Nationwide and may restrict purchases or exchanges of Shares by a Shareholder identified as potentially engaging in transactions that might violate policies established by the Fund.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text weakens the obligation to cooperate by removing "promptly and fully" and making the restriction of trading discretionary ('may restrict') rather than mandatory ('agree to restrict or prohibit'). The phrase "potentially engaging" also adds ambiguity. This creates uncertainty and undermines the Fund's ability to enforce its market timing policies effectively.
perturbation.law_explanation: SEC Rule 22c-2 requires funds to have agreements with intermediaries to restrict trading by shareholders identified as market timers. By making the restriction of trading discretionary, the modified text weakens the agreement and hinders the Fund's ability to meet its obligations under Rule 22c-2.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 270.22c-2 - Redemption fees for redeemable securities. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 270.22c-2 - Redemption fees for redeemable securities. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 270.22c-2 Redemption fees for redeemable securities. (a) Redemption fee. It is unlawful for any fund issuing redeemable securities, its principal underwriter , or any dealer in such securities, to redeem a redeemable security issued by the fund within seven calendar days after the security was purchased, unless it complies with the following requirements: (1) Board determination. The fund 's board of directors, including a majority of directors who are not interested persons of the fund , must either: (i) Approve a redemption fee, in an amount (but no more than two percent of the value of shares redeemed) and on shares redeemed within a time period (but no less than seven calendar days), that in its judgment is necessary or appropriate to recoup for the fund the costs it may incur as a result of those redemptions or to otherwise eliminate or reduce so far as practicable any dilution of the value of the outstanding securities issued by the fund , the proceeds of which fee will be retained by the fund ; or (ii) Determine that imposition of a redemption fee is either not necessary or not appropriate. (2) Shareholder information. With respect to each financial intermediary that submits orders, itself or through its agent, to purchase or redeem shares directly to the fund , its principal underwriter or transfer agent, or to a registered clearing agency, the fund (or on the fund 's behalf, the principal underwriter or transfer agent) must either: (i) Enter into a shareholder information agreement with the financial intermediary (or its agent); or (ii) Prohibit the financial intermediary from purchasing in nominee name on behalf of other persons, securities issued by th
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity and weakens the mandatory nature of restricting trading, potentially hindering compliance with SEC Rule 22c-2. The original text states an agreement to "restrict or prohibit" further purchases, while the changed text uses "may restrict," making the action discretionary. The law explanation states SEC Rule 22c-2 requires funds to have agreements with intermediaries to restrict trading by shareholders identified as market timers. This shift undermines the fund's ability to enforce market timing policies, creating a legal contradiction since compliance is no longer guaranteed by the contract. Scraped Law Snippet 2, 17 CFR § 270.22c-2, discusses redemption fees for redeemable securities, but also refers to shareholder information agreements, supporting the legal framework around regulating shareholder trading behavior and the necessity for clear, enforceable agreements.

perturbation.original_text: You agree to transmit the requested information that is on your books and records to the Fund or its designee promptly, but in any event not later than five (5) business days, after receipt of a request.
perturbation.changed_text: You agree to try to transmit the requested information that is on your books and records to the Fund or its designee within a reasonable time, after receipt of a request.
perturbation.explanation: This change replaces the clear deadline ('promptly, but in any event not later than five (5) business days') with a vague requirement ('within a reasonable time'). This ambiguity makes it difficult for the Fund to enforce the information request provision and assess compliance.
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 22c-2 requires intermediaries to provide information to Funds to enable monitoring of market timing. Specifying a clear timeline for transmitting requested data helps to ensure that the Fund can effectively monitor shareholder trading activity and enforce its policies. Removing the specific timeframe undermines that objective and therefore increases the chance of non-compliance.

CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 270.22c-2 Redemption fees for redeemable securities. (a) Redemption fee. It is unlawful for any fund issuing redeemable securities, its principal underwriter , or any dealer in such securities, to redeem a redeemable security issued by the fund within seven calendar days after the security was purchased, unless it complies with the following requirements: (1) Board determination. The fund 's board of directors, including a majority of directors who are not interested persons of the fund , must either: (i) Approve a redemption fee, in an amount (but no more than two percent of the value of shares redeemed) and on shares redeemed within a time period (but no less than seven calendar days), that in its judgment is necessary or appropriate to recoup for the fund the costs it may incur as a result of those redemptions or to otherwise eliminate or reduce so far as practicable any dilution of the value of the outstanding securities issued by the fund , the proceeds of which fee will be retained by the fund ; or (ii) Determine that imposition of a redemption fee is either not necessary or not appropriate. (2) Shareholder information. With respect to each financial intermediary that submits orders, itself or through its agent, to purchase or redeem shares directly to the fund , its principal underwriter or transfer agent, or to a registered clearing agency, the fund (or on the fund 's behalf, the principal underwriter or transfer agent) must either: (i) Enter into a shareholder information agreement with the financial intermediary (or its agent); or (ii) Prohibit the financial intermediary from purchasing in nominee name on behalf of other persons, securities issued by th
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a clear deadline ('promptly, but in any event not later than five (5) business days') for transmitting information, which aligns with Rule 22c-2's objective of enabling effective monitoring of market timing by Funds. The changed text replaces this with a vague 'reasonable time,' creating ambiguity and hindering enforcement. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is irrelevant, Scraped Law Snippet 2 (17 CFR § 270.22c-2) discusses redemption fees and shareholder information agreements related to monitoring activity. The 'reasonable time' modification weakens the Fund's ability to enforce timely information requests, increasing the risk of non-compliance with regulations intended to facilitate market monitoring.
